zh.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Hansard於1228年之前與Richard Hansard在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Hansard于1228年之前与Richard Hansard在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Hansard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): of Norman origin, from the Middle English and Old French personal name Ansard with prosthetic H-; from ancient Germanic ans- ‘(pagan) god’ + ward ‘guard, protector’ or hard- ‘brave, hardy’.

variants.name: Hazard
meanings: English: Anglo-Norman French variant of   Hansard  .

English and French: nickname from Old French, Middle English hasard, hazard ‘game of dice’, perhaps given to a player at hazard, i.e. a gambler, trickster, or rascal. The word probably derives from Arabic az-zahr, from az, assimilated form of the definite article al, + zahr ‘die’. It appears to have been picked up in the Holy Land and brought back to Europe by Provençal crusaders.
English: possibly in some instances of Flemish origin, a nickname from Middle Dutch hasart ‘hare’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Hansard married Richard Hansard before 1228, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Hansard a épousé Richard Hansard avant 1228, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Hansard hat vor 1228 in England Richard Hansard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Hansard lei sposato Richard Hansard prima del 1228, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Hansardは1228年以前にイギリスでRichard Hansardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Hansard(은)는 1228년 이전 영국에서 Richard Hansard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Hansard casou-se com Richard Hansard antes 1228, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Hansard вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Hansard перед 1228 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard Hansard se casó con Richard Hansard antes de 1228, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija.
